findings.description: The typography hierarchy table (font family per level/channel: Agenda/Nunito Sans/Aptos) and the associated available-weights lists have no corresponding rule at all in the candidate set. This entire font-assignment governance block is missing from the cluster.

payload.description: Font family assignments per hierarchy level and channel (Print/Digital/Word-PPT-Excel) plus available weights per typeface

findings.description: The [GENERAL] email font-stack CSS rule (fallback stack, @font-face loading requirement) is a distinct governance statement not represented by either candidate rule.

payload.description: Email font stack CSS with Nunito Sans primary and Verdana/Helvetica/Arial/sans-serif fallback; must load via @font-face/Google Fonts where supported

findings.description: This single rule merges seven distinct size/line-height specs (H1, H2, H3, body minimum, secondary/supporting, footnote, absolute floor) that carry different hardness/polarity: the 16px body minimum and 'never below 12px' floor are hard mandates, while H1-H3 weight choices ('Bold or SemiBold') and secondary text usage ('use sparingly') are softer guidance. Should be split into at least two rules: a hard type-size-floor rule and a recommended type-scale rule.

splits.description: Never set running text below 12px; body copy minimum 16px
Recommended email heading/subhead/secondary type scale (H1/H2/H3/secondary/footnote sizes and weights)

findings.description: Source explicitly tags these items as [GENERAL], indicating a governance scope facet (general vs brand-specific) that is not captured on the candidate rules.

Unit conflates two rules of different hardness: 'minimum 150% for all body copy' is a hard brand accessibility rule, while the increase to 160% for light-on-dark is a conditional [GENERAL] recommendation ('use 160%'). Clustering them as one hard_constraint rule overstates the hardness/polarity of the conditional guidance.

splits.description: Minimum 150% line height for all body copy
For light text on dark backgrounds, increase line height further, e.g. 160% (16px/26px)

findings.description: The visual-hierarchy governance rule ('never use two adjacent text blocks at same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels') is a distinct, unclustered typography rule missing from the candidate set.

payload.description: Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels
